reasoning: The 30-day base rate of fatalities is ~11.67, requiring >=12 deaths to resolve YES. Because count data is right-skewed, the median is typically lower than the mean, making a random 30-day window have a base probability of ~0.3-0.4 of exceeding the mean. Crucially, the main driver of armed conflict fatalities in Angola—the FLEC-FAC rebellion in Cabinda—declared a two-month unilateral ceasefire starting on April 14, 2025. This ceasefire perfectly overlaps with the first resolution window (ending May 18) and the second (ending June 10), suppressing expected fatalities to well below normal. For the May 18 date, 23 of the 30 days have already passed with no reports of large-scale fatalities, making a sudden spike in the final week highly unlikely (p=0.07). The June window will similarly track the ceasefire (p=0.17). By August, the ceasefire will have expired, returning the odds to the baseline ~0.32 level. For the November date, uncertainty about political and conflict developments pushes the forecast closer to 0.5 (p=0.42).
